PLEX v0.6 presentation report

31 policy kernels.
One five-hook waist.

This atlas shows how every committed PLEX paper replication maps onto admit, route, schedule, cache, and feedback. Each filled cell names the policy authority exercised by the Wasm component; links lead to the paper, implementation, metadata, and deterministic evidence.

31paper policies
31/31passing fixtures
23multi-hook policies
6declared-only cells

Programming model coverage

The stable waist, measured by real policy kernels

The normal path is admit → route → schedule → feedback. Cache is an independent decision boundary for insertion, pressure, expiry, and dependency progress. Click a card to filter the matrix.

Schedule is the broadest authority.

20 policies allocate service, often composed with feedback for fairness or cache for locality.

Admission stays deliberately narrow.

Only 5 kernels need request-entry authority; overload, SLO, quota, and branch-control policies justify the hook.

Feedback depth is visible, not hidden.

9 policies contain custom feedback reducers; 6 feedback surfaces are declared in metadata but remain explicit implementation gaps.

Policy-to-hook matrix

Every implemented paper, mapped cell by cell

A decision cell returns a plan. A state update cell reduces enacted outcomes. A declared only cell appears in replication metadata but has no custom hook reducer in the current component; it is shown as a gap rather than completed semantics.

Must include 31 / 31 policies
Paper policy AdmitWhich proposed requests may enter serving RouteWhere admitted requests should execute ScheduleWhich runnable requests receive service and how much CacheWhich resident or prospective objects remain cached FeedbackHow enacted outcomes update policy state Evidence
Autellix / Agentix Agentix: An Efficient Serving Engine for LLM Agents as General Programs 2026 · NSDI 2026 (final paper: Agentix); arXiv 2025 preprint: Autellix · PLEX wiki -- -- decision

Rank work groups by least attained service, with an anti-starvation boost.

 -- state update

Charge enacted service to request or work-group policy state.

 passing 2 hooks policy-kernel reproduction

Policy kernel

  • group-level least attained service
  • waiting-to-service anti-starvation
  • feedback-maintained group service

Required facts

waiting_msservice_us

Optional mechanics

None

Deferred engine mechanics

  • KV swap kernel
  • multi-step engine execution
  • full ATLAS DAG runtime

Evidence: policy-kernel-reproduction. This reproduces the policy kernel, not the paper's complete serving system.

Paper and implementation

Michael Luo, Xiaoxiang Shi, Colin Cai, Tianjun Zhang, et al.

NSDI 2026 author PDF; catalog citations: 1.

Paper No confirmed artifact Wiki Component Metadata Case Expected trace
Continuum Continuum: Efficient and Robust Multi-Turn LLM Agent Scheduling with KV Cache Time-to-Live 2026 · arXiv preprint / UC Berkeley technical report, revised 2026 · PLEX wiki -- -- decision

Prioritize preempted and TTL-pinned work, then apply group-level FCFS.

 decision

Keep TTL-active beneficiaries resident and reclaim unpinned state.

 state update

Activate or expire per-request TTL state from enacted outcomes.

 passing 3 hooks policy-kernel reproduction

Policy kernel

  • preempted-first priority
  • TTL-pinned priority
  • group-level FCFS
  • TTL-aware cache retention

Required facts

preemptedprogram_arrivalttl_msttl_expiredreload_cost

Optional mechanics

None

Deferred engine mechanics

  • tool parser
  • offline TTL estimator
  • physical KV pinning

Evidence: policy-kernel-reproduction. This reproduces the policy kernel, not the paper's complete serving system.

Paper and implementation

Hanchen Li, Runyuan He, Qiuyang Mang, Qizheng Zhang, et al.

arXiv 2511.02230; catalog citations: not resolved.

Paper Artifact Wiki Component Metadata Case Expected trace
KVFlow KVFlow: Efficient Prefix Caching for Accelerating LLM-Based Multi-Agent Workflows 2025 · NeurIPS 2025 · PLEX wiki -- -- decision

Prioritize requests whose required cache state is already loaded.

 decision

Reclaim varying suffixes first and stage prefetch for marked objects.

 -- passing 2 hooks policy-kernel reproduction

Policy kernel

  • cache-loading-aware scheduling
  • varying-suffix-first reclaim
  • steps-to-execution fixed-prefix ordering
  • prefetch intent

Required facts

cache_readyfixed_prefixsteps_to_executionprefetch

Optional mechanics

cache.prefetch@1

Deferred engine mechanics

  • CPU-GPU transfer overlap

Evidence: policy-kernel-reproduction. This reproduces the policy kernel, not the paper's complete serving system.

uncached_tokenscached_tokensload_costeviction_cost

Paper and implementation

Wei Da, Evangelia Kalyvianaki

arXiv 2606.17949; catalog citations: 0.

Paper No confirmed artifact Wiki Component Metadata Case Expected trace

Read the claim conservatively.

All 31 entries are policy-kernel-reproduction artifacts with deterministic passing fixtures. They demonstrate that the PLEX v0.6 programming model can express each central decision kernel. They do not claim complete source-system performance or physical engine mechanics.

Optional mechanics represented in this corpus: cache.prefetch@1 (3), cache.swap@1 (1), request.cancel@1 (4), request.rebalance@1 (6)
